import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  mut num = 0
  mut sign = 1
  mut building = false
  while i <= n
    mut c = 32
    if i < n
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(v)
        c = v
      when None
        c = 32
      end
    end
    if c >= 48 and c <= 57
      num = num * 10 + (c - 48)
      building = true
    else if c == 45 and not building
      sign = -1
      building = true
    else
      if building
        result = list.push(result, sign * num)
      end
      num = 0
      sign = 1
      building = false
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

fn get_at(_ xs: List<Int>, _ i: Int) -> Int
  match xs.get(i)
  when Some(v)
    v
  when None
    0
  end
end

fn find_and_print(_ nums: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Unit
  let n = nums.len()
  mut i = 0
  mut found = false
  mut fi = 0
  mut fj = 0
  while i < n and not found
    mut j = i + 1
    while j < n and not found
      let a = get_at(nums, i)
      let b = get_at(nums, j)
      if a + b == t
        found = true
        fi = i
        fj = j
      end
      j = j + 1
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if found
    print("found=#{fi},#{fj}")
  else
    print("none")
  end
end

match io.read_line()
when Some(l1)
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(l2)
    let nums = parse_ints(l1)
    match string.parse_int(string.trim(l2))
    when Some(t)
      find_and_print(nums, t)
    when None
      print("none")
    end
  when None
    print("none")
  end
when None
  print("none")
end
